title: Al-Shabaab Mortar Attack on Halane Base Camp Near Mogadishu Airport; AU Forces Among Casualties
detail: Al-Shabaab conducted a mortar attack targeting Halane base camp, the highly fortified international security zone adjacent to Aden Adde International Airport in Mogadishu, on April 28, 2026. AU soldiers stationed at Halane were among those killed or wounded in the strike. Halane camp serves as the headquarters of the AU Support and Stabilization Mission in Somalia (AUSSOM) and also houses UN agencies, Western diplomatic missions, and international contractors — making it one of the highest-profile targets in the Somali capital. Mortar attacks on Halane represent a signature Al-Shabaab tactic to demonstrate its ability to reach internationally protected zones and to demoralize international partners. The attack came as Somalia's constitutional crisis enters its penultimate phase with 17 days until President Mohamud's May 15 term expiry, Al-Shabaab continues to hold Adan Yabal in Hiiraan (seized April 19), and the SNA/Macawisley counter-offensive remains stalled. The Halane strike signals that Al-Shabaab is maintaining offensive pressure across multiple vectors simultaneously — targeting elite forces and international presence in Mogadishu even as it consolidates territorial gains in Hiiraan Region. Full casualty figures were not immediately confirmed pending assessment.

media.caption: Al-Shabaab mortar attack kills AU soldiers at Halane base camp near Mogadishu airport, April 28, 2026

title: SNA Forces Seize Al-Shabaab Hideout in Middle Shabelle, Defense Ministry Confirms
detail: Somali National Army forces conducted a targeted ground operation in Middle Shabelle Region on April 28, 2026, seizing an Al-Shabaab hideout and disrupting the group's operational infrastructure along the Shabelle River corridor. The SNA Defense Ministry confirmed the operation, which yielded weapons caches and dismantled a forward position used to stage attacks against government-held towns. Middle Shabelle has been a persistent flashpoint in 2026: the NISA and international partners killed 33 militants in a two-phase operation at Guulane village on April 22, and the SNA's Gorgor Commando had earlier neutralized cells at War-Iise in March. The Shabelle corridor is critical as Al-Shabaab uses it for supply, financing, and staging attacks toward Mogadishu. The Middle Shabelle operation comes alongside the stalled SNA/Macawisley counter-offensive to retake Adan Yabal in Hiiraan and ongoing Operation Bader pressure in Lower Shabelle — indicating SNA is attempting to maintain multi-front pressure despite the political crisis in Mogadishu and ongoing international community concerns about the sustainability of the security transition.

media.caption: SNA forces seize Al-Shabaab hideout in Middle Shabelle on April 28, 2026 as military maintains pressure along the Shabelle River corridor
